
```markdown
---
title: "Bundesliga: Bayern's Iron Grip"
subtitle: "32 seasons of German football's competitive imbalance"
author: "Eric Schnitger"
date: 2026-04-27
categories: [data-visualization, python, sports-analytics, web-scraping]
image: thumbnail.png
description: |
 An interactive analysis of every Bundesliga season from 1992 to 2025,
 examining Bayern Munich's structural dominance and its effect on
 competitive balance.
format:
 html:
   code-fold: true
   code-summary: "Show code"
   code-tools: true
   toc: true
   toc-depth: 3
execute:
 echo: true
 warning: false
 freeze: auto
---

## The Question

Bayern Munich have won the Bundesliga more than any other club in history. But
has their dominance grown, or has it always been this extreme? And with the
50+1 rule supposedly ensuring competitive balance, why does Germany's league
look so predictable?

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Summary

- **Bayern have won ~21 of 32 titles** (66%) since 1992
- **The gap trend is flatter than the Premier League** because Bayern's dominance was already established
- **Dortmund's emergence** (2010-12) represents the only sustained challenge
- **The 18-team, 34-match format** produces lower raw point totals, but the ratio of champion to survival is comparable to other leagues
- **16th-place playoff** makes the relegation boundary more ambiguous than in other leagues
:::

```{python}
#| label: load-data-bundesliga
#| code-summary: "Import and load data"

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

from _utils import (
   LEAGUES, scrape_league, enrich_dataframe,
   build_league_chart, build_frequency_tables,
)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

config = LEAGUES["bundesliga"]
df = scrape_league("bundesliga", cache_dir=".")
df = enrich_dataframe(df)

print(f"Dataset: {len(df)} seasons loaded")
df[["Season", "Champion", "Title-Winning Points", "Survived Relegation",
   "Relegation Survival Points", "Relegated 1", "Relegated 2", "Relegated 3"]].tail(10)
```

## Interactive Analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-main-bundesliga
#| fig-cap: "Bundesliga: Title-winning vs. relegation survival points"
#| code-summary: "Build visualization"

eras = [
   {"label": "Full History (1992-2025)", "start": df["Season"].iloc[0],
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Bundesliga: Title vs. Relegation (1992-2025)"},
   {"label": "Pre-Guardiola (1992-2013)", "start": "1992/93", "end": "2012/13",
    "title": "Bundesliga: Traditional Era (1992-2013)"},
   {"label": "Modern Bayern (2013-2025)", "start": "2013/14",
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Bundesliga: Modern Bayern (2013-2025)"},
]

outliers = [
   {"season": "2012/13", "y": 91, "text": "Bayern Treble",
    "color": "#EF5350", "ax": 0, "ay": -25},
]

# Fix y-values from actual data
for outlier in outliers:
   match = df[df["Season"] == outlier["season"]]
   if not match.empty and outlier["y"] is None:
       outlier["y"] = int(match["Title-Winning Points"].iloc[0])

fig = build_league_chart(df, config, eras=eras, outliers=outliers)
fig.show()
```

## Champions League Concentration

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top4
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequent Bundesliga Top 4 finishers"
#| code-summary: "Top 4 frequency"

top4_clubs, relegated_clubs = build_frequency_tables(df, config)
top4_clubs.head(10).style.format({"Percentage": "{:.1f}%"}).hide(axis="index")
```

## Relegated Clubs

```{python}
#| label: tbl-relegated
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequently relegated Bundesliga clubs"
#| code-summary: "Relegation frequency"

relegated_clubs.head(10).style.hide(axis="index")
```

## Context

- **18 teams**, 34 matches per season (lower point totals than 20-team leagues)
- **2 direct relegation + 1 playoff** (16th place plays 3rd in 2. Bundesliga)
- **50+1 ownership rule** prevents majority private ownership, yet Bayern's commercial dominance achieves a similar competitive imbalance through wages
- **Dortmund** (2011, 2012) and **Wolfsburg** (2009) are rare interlopers

---

*Part of [The European Football Divide](../) project.*
```
